__data__.text:
ZOIKOLOGIA™

ASK KRITON™ QUERY ORCHESTRATION

Canonical MVP Workflow — Engineering Specification

| Field             | Value                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Document ID       | ZL-ENG-02 v1.0                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Platform          | ZoikoLogia™ — Kriton™ advisor / Massarius™ engine                                                                                                                                       |
| Subject           | Canonical orchestration workflow for Ask Kriton™ query handling: validation, safety screening, source retrieval, risk classification, routing, composition, audit and response contract |
| Status            | Proposed — approved with release gates and implementation conditions in Section 13                                                                                                      |
| Prepared for      | AI Engineering, Product, UI, QA, Security, Compliance and Documentation Teams                                                                                                           |
| Related documents | ZL-BRAND-01 v1.1; ZL-ENG-01 v1.0                                                                                                                                                        |
| Classification    | Confidential — internal engineering and product specification                                                                                                                           |

Specification of Record

This document supersedes the submitted engineer workflow as the canonical MVP orchestration specification for Ask Kriton™. The submitted flow is retained as the structural baseline, but the corrected controls in this document are mandatory before release.

## 1. Executive Assessment

The submitted engineer workflow — query, retrieve, classify, compose, audit and respond — is a valid MVP skeleton. It is not sufficient as a production-facing specification for a source-governed, multi-tenant accounting intelligence platform.

This specification closes the material gaps that would otherwise create engineering ambiguity, compliance exposure, audit weakness or unsafe model behaviour.

| Correction                   | Implementation Requirement                                                                                                                |
|------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Routing ambiguity            | Every risk and confidence combination now maps to exactly one route through a versioned policy matrix.                                    |
| Audit integrity conflict     | Fire-and-forget audit writes are not permitted for chain-hashed evidence. Ordered durable persistence is required before response return. |
| Pre-screen terminal handling | Pre-screen failure short-circuits the pipeline. No retrieval or model work runs after a failed safety pre-screen.

Ordered durable persistence is required before response return. |
| Pre-screen terminal handling | Pre-screen failure short-circuits the pipeline. No retrieval or model work runs after a failed safety pre-screen.                         |
| Output-side validation       | LLM output must pass grounding, citation binding and prohibited-claim checks before it can be returned.                                   |
| Tenant isolation             | Source retrieval must be tenant-scoped at the data-access layer, not merely filtered in application code.                                 |
| Frontend safety              | The frontend receives opaque audit references only. Internal hashes, policy internals and audit chain material are not exposed.           |

## 2. Canonical Operating Principles

| Principle                | Rule                                                                                                       |
|--------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| User-facing advisor      | Kriton™ is the only user-facing advisor name. The entry point is Ask Kriton™.                              |
| Engine layer             | Massarius™ is the engine layer name used in architecture, technical and governance documentation.          |
| No unsupported answering | A safe query with inadequate source support must not be answered from general model knowledge.             |
| Policy before model      | The route decision controls whether the model gateway may run. The model never re-decides a blocked route. |
| Audit before response    | No substantive response is returned without the corresponding durable audit trail.                         |
| Deterministic frontend   | The frontend renders from route and outcome fields, not by interpreting answer text.                       |

## 3. Canonical Flow

User submits query via Ask Kriton™
  |
  |  POST /api/v1/orchestration/ask  (Idempotency-Key required)
  v
orchestration/service.py
  |
  +-- 1. Generate identifiers
  |      query\_id, correlation\_id, request\_id, audit\_chain\_id
  |      tenant\_id and user\_id/session\_id resolved from auth context
  |
  +-- AUDIT: query\_received
  |
  +-- 2.

Validate request
  |      schema, tenant entitlement, permissions, rate limit, idempotency
  |      FAIL -> route = REJECTED -> AUDIT: request\_rejected -> return
  |
  +-- AUDIT: request\_validated
  |
  +-- 3. Pre-screen safety before retrieval
  |      prompt injection, malicious instruction, data exfiltration, unsupported request
  |      FAIL -> route = SECURITY\_INCIDENT or REFUSAL -> persist incident/refusal -> return
  |
  +-- AUDIT: pre\_safety\_screen\_completed
  |
  +-- 4. Retrieve SourceBundle [Massarius™ retrieval layer]
  |      orchestration/retrieve.py
  |      MVP: category-keyword source selection; pre-RAG; do not label as RAG
  |      tenant-scoped source\_library query with tenant\_id enforced at data layer
  |      returns SourceBundle, confidence\_state, exclusion\_reasons
  |      FAIL -> route = CLARIFICATION or REFUSAL
  |
  +-- AUDIT: retrieval\_started + retrieval\_completed / retrieval\_failed
  |
  +-- 5. Classify professional risk [risk\_safety]
  |      LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / RESTRICTED
  |      route selected from versioned policy matrix
  |      classifier\_version and policy\_version recorded
  |
  +-- AUDIT: risk\_classified + route\_selected
  |
  +-- 6.

Execute deterministic route
  |      LLM -> model\_gateway (MVP: MockProviderAdapter.complete())
  |      REFUSAL -> safe refusal response
  |      HUMAN\_REVIEW -> persisted review\_case object
  |      CLARIFICATION -> bounded clarifying question
  |      SECURITY\_INCIDENT -> persisted incident object and minimal safe response
  |
  +-- AUDIT: route\_execution\_completed / composition\_failed
  |
  +-- 7. Post-composition validation (LLM route only)
  |      grounding check, citation binding, prohibited-claim scan
  |      FAIL -> composition\_rejected -> route degrades to REFUSAL or HUMAN\_REVIEW
  |
  +-- 8. Post-process response
  |      citations, confidence\_state, safety state, disclaimers, redaction of internals
  |
  +-- AUDIT: response\_finalised + response\_returned
  |
  v
Structured response contract returned to frontend

## 4. Endpoint and Request Envelope

The orchestration endpoint receives a user query submitted through Ask Kriton™ and returns a route-driven response contract. The endpoint must be idempotent, tenant-aware and audit-bound.

POST /api/v1/orchestration/ask
Required header: Idempotency-Key: <client-generated-key>

| Control                | Requirement                                                                                                                                                              |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Authentication context | tenant_id, user_id, session_id and permissions are resolved from the authenticated context. They are never trusted from request body fields.                             |
| Idempotency            | Duplicate submissions with the same idempotency key return the original result or current terminal state. They do not re-execute retrieval, model calls or audit chains. |
| Rate limiting          | Rate limits are evaluated before retrieval or model work.                                                                                                                |
| Payload validation     | Malformed payloads return route = REJECTED and outcome = rejected. They are audited.

|
| Rate limiting          | Rate limits are evaluated before retrieval or model work.                                                                                                                |
| Payload validation     | Malformed payloads return route = REJECTED and outcome = rejected. They are audited.                                                                                     |

## 5. Identifier Model

| Identifier           | Purpose                                                                                         |
|----------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| query_id             | Business-level ID for the user-submitted query lifecycle.                                       |
| correlation_id       | Cross-service trace ID spanning orchestration, retrieval, risk_safety, model_gateway and audit. |
| request_id           | HTTP/API request instance ID.                                                                   |
| audit_chain_id       | Audit ledger chain reference for this query lifecycle.                                          |
| tenant_id            | Customer/workspace boundary; resolved from auth context; never accepted from the payload.       |
| user_id / session_id | User and session attribution resolved from auth context.                                        |
| idempotency_key      | Client-supplied key used to prevent duplicate execution.                                        |

MVP concession

For MVP only, query\_id may be reused as correlation\_id if documented in code and audit metadata. In production, query\_id, correlation\_id, request\_id and audit\_chain\_id must be separately generated and explicitly mapped.

## 6. Validation and Pre-Screen Safety

Request validation and pre-screen safety run before source retrieval. This ordering is a release gate.

| Check                    | Required Behaviour                                                                                       |
|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Schema validation        | Reject invalid payload shape, unsupported fields, empty query text or unsupported content type.          |
| Tenant entitlement       | Confirm tenant is active, authorised and entitled to the requested capability.                           |
| Permission check         | Confirm the user/session may invoke Ask Kriton™ for the tenant and workspace.                            |
| Rate limit               | Apply user, tenant and endpoint limits before retrieval.                                                 |
| Prompt injection screen  | Detect attempts to override system rules, reveal internals or ignore source governance.                  |
| Data exfiltration screen | Detect attempts to access unauthorised tenant data, hidden sources, credentials, logs or system prompts.

|
| Data exfiltration screen | Detect attempts to access unauthorised tenant data, hidden sources, credentials, logs or system prompts. |
| Terminal failure         | A failed pre-screen maps to SECURITY_INCIDENT or REFUSAL and short-circuits the pipeline.                |

## 7. Retrieval Layer: Massarius™ Source Retrieval MVP

The current retrieval layer is a category-keyword source-selection stand-in. It is pre-RAG. It validates source eligibility, safety routing and audit flow before semantic retrieval is introduced.

The term “RAG” must not be used in code comments, documentation or external materials until embeddings, chunking, semantic retrieval, ranking, re-ranking, citation binding, retrieval evaluation, hallucination checks and source freshness handling are implemented.

### 7.1 Tenant Isolation

Every source\_library query must carry tenant\_id at the data-access layer. Application-level filtering alone is not sufficient. Row-level security or an equivalent lower-level enforcement mechanism is required where available.

Release Gate

A retrieval integration test proving tenant isolation is required before release. Cross-tenant source leakage is a blocking defect.

### 7.2 SourceBundle Schema

{
  "source\_bundle\_id": "sb\_...",
  "eligible\_sources": [],
  "excluded\_sources": [],
  "exclusion\_reasons": [],
  "jurisdiction": "UK | US | IFRS | ...",
  "source\_types": ["standard", "guidance", "internal\_policy"],
  "authority\_level": "primary | secondary | internal",
  "freshness\_state": "current | stale | unknown",
  "licence\_state": "permitted | restricted | unknown",
  "retrieval\_method": "keyword\_mvp",
  "confidence\_state": "sufficient | limited | insufficient | conflicting\_sources | stale\_sources | restricted\_sources"
}

| Confidence State    | Behaviour                                                                                 |
|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| sufficient          | Source foundation is adequate for answer composition if risk route permits.

|
| limited             | Answer may proceed only where policy permits; caveats and disclaimers are mandatory.      |
| insufficient        | Do not answer from general model knowledge. Route to clarification or refusal per matrix. |
| conflicting_sources | Escalate to human review. Preserve conflict summary for reviewer.                         |
| stale_sources       | Clarify or refuse where current authority is essential.                                   |
| restricted_sources  | Refuse. Restricted content must not be exposed through the answer.                        |

## 8. Deterministic Risk Classification and Routing

Routes are selected only from the versioned policy matrix below. The matrix is the single source of truth. Each route\_selected audit event records classifier\_version and policy\_version, making the decision reproducible.

| Risk Level         | Confidence State       | Route             | Behaviour                                                         |
|--------------------|------------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------|
| LOW                | sufficient             | LLM               | Compose answer with citations.                                    |
| LOW                | limited                | LLM               | Compose with mandatory caveats and disclaimer.                    |
| LOW                | insufficient           | CLARIFICATION     | Ask for jurisdiction, reporting framework, entity type or scope.  |
| MEDIUM             | sufficient             | LLM               | Compose with disclaimer_required = true.                          |
| MEDIUM             | limited / insufficient | HUMAN_REVIEW      | Create review case; return escalation state.                      |
| HIGH               | any                    | HUMAN_REVIEW      | Professional judgement escalation. Do not compose final advice.   |
| RESTRICTED         | any                    | REFUSAL           | Safe refusal; no composition.                                     |
| any                | conflicting_sources    | HUMAN_REVIEW      | Escalate; conflict summary included in review case.               |
| any                | stale_sources          | CLARIFICATION     | Warn or ask for updated context; refuse if freshness is critical. |
| any                | restricted_sources     | REFUSAL           | Restricted source content must not be exposed.

|
| any                | restricted_sources     | REFUSAL           | Restricted source content must not be exposed.                    |
| Pre-screen failure | n/a                    | SECURITY_INCIDENT | Persist incident; return minimal safe response; no retrieval.     |

### 8.1 Route Rules

- The model gateway executes only when route == LLM.
- Clarification is bounded to two cycles per query\_id. The third unresolved cycle escalates to HUMAN\_REVIEW.
- HUMAN\_REVIEW creates a persisted review\_case object; returning an escalation label without a persisted object is non-compliant.
- SECURITY\_INCIDENT creates a persisted incident object with the triggering classification, correlation\_id and evidence reference.
- REFUSAL returns a safe user-facing message and records refusal\_returned in the audit chain.

## 9. Model Gateway and Composition Controls

The model gateway sits behind a provider abstraction. The MVP implementation may use MockProviderAdapter.complete(), but the interface must be production-shaped from the start.

| Control              | Requirement                                                                                                            |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Provider abstraction | All provider calls pass through one gateway interface. No direct provider calls from orchestration modules.            |
| Timeout              | Each call has a bounded timeout and safe failure state.                                                                |
| Retries              | Retries are bounded and use backoff. Retried calls must not duplicate audit events incorrectly.                        |
| Circuit breaker      | Provider instability opens a circuit and returns a safe composition_failed state.                                      |
| No partial leakage   | Stack traces, raw prompts, system prompts, internal policy text and partial unsafe output must not reach the frontend. |
| Source grounding     | Prompt context is limited to the approved SourceBundle and permitted metadata.                                         |

## 10. Post-Composition Validation

Route gating before composition is not sufficient. LLM output must pass an output-side gate before response finalisation.

| Validation                  | Required Behaviour                                                                                                          |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Grounding check             | Every substantive answer claim must be attributable to the SourceBundle. Unattributable answers fail validation.

| Validation                  | Required Behaviour                                                                                                          |
|-----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Grounding check             | Every substantive answer claim must be attributable to the SourceBundle. Unattributable answers fail validation.            |
| Citation binding            | Citations must reference sources actually present in eligible_sources.                                                      |
| Prohibited-claim scan       | Output is checked against ZL-ENG-01 prohibited claims and professional-use restrictions.                                    |
| Disclosure/disclaimer check | Required disclaimers must be attached for MEDIUM risk or limited confidence responses.                                      |
| Failure path                | On failure, audit composition_rejected and route degrades to REFUSAL or HUMAN_REVIEW. The invalid answer is never returned. |

## 11. Persisted Objects for Escalation and Incident Handling

### 11.1 Human Review Object

{
  "review\_case\_id": "hr\_123",
  "query\_id": "qry\_123",
  "tenant\_id": "ten\_123",
  "risk\_level": "HIGH",
  "confidence\_state": "conflicting\_sources",
  "reason": "Professional judgement or source conflict requires review",
  "assigned\_queue": "accounting\_review",
  "status": "open",
  "created\_at": "ISO 8601 UTC"
}

### 11.2 Security Incident Object

{
  "incident\_id": "sec\_123",
  "query\_id": "qry\_123",
  "correlation\_id": "corr\_123",
  "tenant\_id": "ten\_123",
  "trigger": "prompt\_injection | data\_exfiltration | malicious\_instruction",
  "evidence\_reference": "encrypted\_ref\_123",
  "status": "open",
  "created\_at": "ISO 8601 UTC"
}

## 12. Frontend Response Contract

The frontend renders from route and outcome. It must not infer state by parsing answer text. One contract shape is used for all routes; unused fields are null.

The frontend receives only the opaque audit\_chain\_id.

### 12.1 Clarification Example

{
  "query\_id": "qry\_123",
  "correlation\_id": "corr\_123",
  "outcome": "clarification\_required",
  "route": "CLARIFICATION",
  "safety": {
    "risk\_level": "LOW",
    "policy\_state": "needs\_more\_context",
    "disclaimer\_required": false
  },
  "confidence\_state": "insufficient",
  "source\_bundle": null,
  "answer": null,
  "next\_action": {
    "type": "ask\_clarifying\_question",
    "message": "Does this question relate to IFRS or UK GAAP?"
  },
  "audit\_reference": { "audit\_chain\_id": "aud\_123" }
}

## 13. Audit Ledger

The audit ledger is a trust-control system, not operational logging. It must provide durable, ordered, tamper-evident evidence for every query lifecycle.

| Requirement                | Implementation Rule                                                                                                                           |
|----------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Durable ordered writes     | Audit events are persisted synchronously or through a durable ordered queue with confirmed enqueue before the response returns.               |
| Fail-safe behaviour        | If the audit write path is unavailable, no answer is returned. The request fails safe.                                                        |
| Success and failure events | Both successful steps and failed/terminal paths are recorded.                                                                                 |
| Canonical hashing          | event_hash uses SHA-256 over versioned canonical serialisation with stable field order, UTF-8 and previous_event_hash.                        |
| Chain verification         | A scheduled verifier walks audit_chain_id sequences and alerts on breaks.                                                                     |
| External anchoring         | Periodic anchoring of latest chain heads to a separate write-once store is required for production.                                           |
| Personal data              | Raw query text and personal data are not persisted in plaintext. Store content hashes plus encrypted content references under access control.

|
| Personal data              | Raw query text and personal data are not persisted in plaintext. Store content hashes plus encrypted content references under access control. |

### 13.1 Required Audit Events

query\_received
request\_validated
request\_rejected
pre\_safety\_screen\_completed
retrieval\_started
retrieval\_completed
retrieval\_failed
risk\_classified
route\_selected
composition\_started
composition\_completed
composition\_failed
composition\_rejected
human\_review\_created
refusal\_returned
clarification\_returned
security\_incident\_recorded
response\_finalised
response\_returned

### 13.2 Event Schema

{
  "event\_id": "evt\_123",
  "audit\_chain\_id": "aud\_123",
  "correlation\_id": "corr\_123",
  "query\_id": "qry\_123",
  "tenant\_id": "ten\_123",
  "step": "risk\_classified",
  "actor": "system",
  "timestamp": "ISO 8601 UTC",
  "classifier\_version": "rc\_1.0",
  "policy\_version": "pm\_1.0",
  "input\_hash": "...",
  "output\_hash": "...",
  "previous\_event\_hash": "...",
  "event\_hash": "...",
  "metadata": {}
}

## 14. Observability, Testing and Release Gates

### 14.1 Observability

| Area         | Requirement                                                                                                                                                        |
|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Traceability | correlation_id must appear in orchestration, retrieval, risk_safety, model_gateway and audit service logs.                                                         |
| Metrics      | Track route counts, refusal rates, clarification rates, human-review rates, retrieval confidence distribution, composition rejection rate and audit write latency. |
| Alerts       | Alert on audit write failure, tenant-isolation test failure, spike in security incidents, source retrieval errors and model gateway circuit breaker state.         |

### 14.2 Minimum Test Coverage

- Pre-screen failure short-circuits retrieval and model execution.

|

### 14.2 Minimum Test Coverage

- Pre-screen failure short-circuits retrieval and model execution.
- Every policy matrix row resolves to the expected route.
- Tenant A cannot retrieve Tenant B sources under any query path.
- A model output with unsupported claims triggers composition\_rejected.
- A citation not present in eligible\_sources triggers composition\_rejected.
- Clarification escalates after two unresolved cycles.
- Audit write failure prevents answer return.
- Duplicate idempotency key returns the original terminal result, not a second execution.
- Frontend renders route/outcome states without parsing answer text.

### 14.3 Release Gates

| Gate   | Blocking Requirement                                                                                                                   |
|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| RG-01  | Request validation and pre-screen safety run before retrieval, with terminal failure routes.                                           |
| RG-02  | Tenant-scoped retrieval enforced at data-access layer and proven by integration test.                                                  |
| RG-03  | Post-composition validation implemented with composition_rejected path.                                                                |
| RG-04  | Audit ledger supports durable ordered writes before response return, canonical hashing, failure events and no plaintext personal data. |

## 15. Approval Conditions

1. Ask Kriton™ remains the user-facing entry point; orchestration/service.py remains the orchestration controller.
2. retrieve.py is labelled as MVP keyword retrieval; the term RAG is prohibited until Section 7 criteria are met.
3. Request validation and pre-screen safety execute before retrieval, with terminal failure routes.
4. Tenant-scoped retrieval is enforced at the data-access layer with a release-gating isolation test.
5. SourceBundle is implemented per Section 7.2 and confidence\_state drives routing.
6. Routing is implemented from the versioned policy matrix in Section 8; classifier\_version and policy\_version are recorded for each decision.
7. Model gateway executes only on route == LLM and includes timeout, retry, circuit breaker and refusal-safe failure.
8. Post-composition validation is implemented with composition\_rejected path.
9. HUMAN\_REVIEW and SECURITY\_INCIDENT create persisted objects.
10.

8. Post-composition validation is implemented with composition\_rejected path.
9. HUMAN\_REVIEW and SECURITY\_INCIDENT create persisted objects.
10. Audit ledger meets Section 13 requirements.
11. Frontend consumes the Section 12 contract and renders from route/outcome only.

Release blocking conditions

Conditions 3, 4, 8 and 10 are release gates. The remaining conditions are implementation requirements verified through code review, QA evidence and architecture review.

## 16. Final CTO Verdict

Approved as the canonical MVP specification subject to the release gates and approval conditions above. The engineer’s submitted workflow is retained as the baseline orchestration spine, but the corrected specification of record is this document.

No production or customer-facing release should proceed unless deterministic routing, tenant-isolated retrieval, post-composition validation and durable audit integrity are demonstrably implemented and tested.
